decision.overall_recommendation: VIX gate is open, so new swing entries are permissible, but this run's technical screener (pullback-to-rising-200EMA) is a candidate filter only, not a validated edge — selection here leans heavily on fundamentals, earnings-beat patterns, and news catalysts rather than the chart pattern alone. Track record data for this system is insufficient (sample_size=4, below the 10-trade minimum), so no performance-based conviction adjustment is applied, but position sizing should stay disciplined given the small account ($1,735.57 net liq) and 4% per-trade risk. Favor GDS, SUPN, LCII, CTRE, SBRA, HLF, GCT, LXU and DAKT at the top of the list for their consistent beat records and constructive catalysts; treat TDW, GBX, SBCF, VFC, ADNT, MDU, OLN, HUN and CSWC as lower-conviction, monitor-closely candidates given recent misses, deteriorating income trends, merger uncertainty, or a below-threshold R:R (CSWC). Several picks have earnings inside the next ~2-4 weeks, which can invalidate stops/targets before the pattern plays out — size and manage accordingly. Excluded from the top 18 despite passing the screener: HP, NE, EYPT, AHCO and HE, due to recent multi-quarter earnings-miss patterns, deteriorating fundamentals, or a notably bearish analyst skew conflicting with the technical setup.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: GDS has beaten EPS estimates in essentially every quarter over the trailing two years, including a huge Q1 FY2026 beat (1.53 vs -0.03 est.), with revenue growth accelerating (+15.2% most recent quarter) on strong AI data-center demand. Recent headlines flag margin pressure from rising utility costs and FY2026 guidance trailing consensus, tempering the otherwise strong story. Analyst consensus is Buy (17 buy/1 strong buy vs 2 hold).
ranked_picks.rationale: Best combination of consistent beat record, real revenue growth, and a durable AI-demand catalyst; guidance/margin caveats keep it from a pure 'positive' read but don't offset the fundamental strength.
flags: StopSanityFlag true (RR 21.27 driven by an unusually tight stop, not an exceptional target)
Earnings in 37 days - past most of the swing window
PriceVsPOCPct -7.65% (below POC, decent volume support underneath)

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Supernus has beaten EPS estimates in all 8 trailing reported quarters, several by wide margins (Q4 2025: 0.92 vs 0.28 est.), with revenue growth accelerating. The Sage Therapeutics acquisition (Zurzuvae) is a real portfolio-expanding catalyst, but a failed SPN-820 depression trial and a prior Piper Sandler downgrade on slowing Qelbree growth are genuine negatives in the news mix.
ranked_picks.rationale: Exceptionally clean beat record and accretive M&A outweigh the pipeline setback and downgrade, which are real but dated.
flags: Earnings in 22 days - could hit before target/stop resolve

ranked_picks.research_highlight: LCI Industries has beaten EPS estimates in every one of the last 6 reported quarters, and the most recent quarter showed accelerating revenue (+16.9%) and net income growth (+237%). The Bigfoot Hydraulic Systems acquisition extends its RV/utility trailer reach, and recent coverage flags it as an oversold name in consumer discretionary.
ranked_picks.rationale: Clean, uninterrupted beat streak plus accelerating fundamentals and a bolt-on acquisition make this one of the strongest fundamental stories on the list.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: CareTrust REIT's revenue growth has been strong and accelerating (Q4 2025 +29.5%, Q3 +21.2%), though net income growth turned negative in the most recent quarter (-27.9%) and EPS missed slightly in three quarters before the last two beats. Analyst rating is A/Buy (13 buy, 1 strong buy). News flow is routine with no negative catalysts.
ranked_picks.rationale: Top-tier revenue growth and a strong Buy consensus offset a soft net-income quarter; healthcare REIT with no negative headline risk.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Sabra Health Care has beaten or met EPS estimates in every one of the last 8 quarters, including a large Q1 2026 beat (0.39 vs 0.17 est.), with revenue growth positive in 3 of the last 4 quarters. News flow is routine (analyst previews, CNBC 'Final Trades' mentions) with no negative catalysts.
ranked_picks.rationale: Very consistent beat/met record in a defensive healthcare-REIT with no red flags in the news.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Herbalife has beaten EPS estimates in 7 of the last 8 quarters. The standout catalyst: Cristiano Ronaldo's acquisition of a 10% stake in Herbalife's Pro2col wellness platform sent shares up ~14-15% after hours. Analyst consensus is Buy (15 buy, 1 sell).
ranked_picks.rationale: Strong beat record plus a genuine, market-moving positive catalyst.
flags: PriceVsPOCPct +5.22% (above POC - thinner volume support beneath current price)

ranked_picks.research_highlight: GigaCloud has beaten EPS estimates in 6 of the last 8 quarters, with revenue growth positive but decelerating into the most recent quarter (-0.9%). The stock got caught in a broader AI-stock selloff in late 2025 despite otherwise solid fundamentals. Analyst rating is A/Buy.
ranked_picks.rationale: Solid beat history and Buy rating, tempered by decelerating growth and sector-wide AI selloff exposure.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: LSB Industries has posted positive revenue and triple-digit net income growth in all four trailing quarters, and beaten EPS estimates in 4 of the last 6 quarters, including a large recent beat. News flow is sparse and routine with no major catalysts either way.
ranked_picks.rationale: Genuinely improving fundamentals across every recent quarter, though the lack of a specific catalyst and thin news coverage keep it mid-pack.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Daktronics beat EPS estimates in the most recent quarter with a record $356M backlog and strong sales growth (+14.7% revenue, +179.6% net income) reported; a new analyst initiated coverage bullishly. The trailing record is mixed (5 of 8 beats) with a rough Q3 FY2026 miss, but the latest print and backlog commentary are clearly positive. Analyst rating is A-/Buy.
ranked_picks.rationale: Most recent quarter and backlog commentary are strongly positive, and earnings are 58 days out, reducing near-term event risk.
flags: Earnings in 58 days - lower near-term earnings risk than most picks

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Tidewater missed EPS estimates badly in each of its last two reported quarters (0.12 vs 0.75 est.; 0.33 vs 0.75 est.) after three prior beats, and net income growth cratered (-97% most recent quarter). The $500M all-cash acquisition of Wilson Sons Ultratug/Atlantic Offshore to deepen its Brazil offshore push is a real positive catalyst. Analyst consensus is Hold (16 hold, 6 buy, 4 sell).
ranked_picks.rationale: Recent earnings deterioration is a real concern, but the offshore expansion deal and historically decent margins keep it from a clean exclude; ranked mid-pack given the two-quarter miss streak.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Greenbrier has beaten EPS estimates in 6 of the last 8 quarters, but revenue has declined in each of the last four reported quarters and net income growth was negative in three of four. News flow is routine (earnings previews/recaps) with no major catalyst noted.
ranked_picks.rationale: Decent beat record offset by a genuine multi-quarter revenue decline; next earnings is 106 days out, meaningfully reducing near-term event risk for this swing trade.
flags: Earnings in 106 days - low near-term earnings risk

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Seacoast Banking has beaten EPS estimates in 5 of the last 8 quarters, but net income growth was negative in three of the last four quarters despite generally positive revenue. News is routine earnings coverage with no material catalysts.
ranked_picks.rationale: Steady regional bank with a decent but unspectacular beat record and no notable catalyst either way.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: VF Corp's most recent quarter showed a large EPS miss (-0.30 vs -0.006 est.) after four straight beats. The turnaround narrative (North Face strength offsetting Vans weakness, $600M Dickies divestiture, reiterated cash-flow guidance) is real, but a recent analyst turned bearish on the name. Consensus rating is Hold.
ranked_picks.rationale: Genuine turnaround progress in prior quarters undercut by the most recent miss and a fresh bearish downgrade; ranked mid-lower given the conflicting signals.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Adient beat EPS estimates in the two most recent quarters (5 of last 8 overall), with the latest quarter showing a strong rebound in net income growth (+222.7%). Recent news specifically cites 'demand volatility' hurting Q4 results. Analyst consensus is Hold.
ranked_picks.rationale: Recent beats and improving net income are constructive, but explicit demand-volatility commentary and a very large stated RR warrant caution.
flags: StopSanityFlag true (RR 33.58 likely reflects an unusually tight stop rather than an outsized target)
PriceVsPOCPct -15.31% (well below POC - thicker volume support underneath)

ranked_picks.research_highlight: MDU Resources' revenue and net income figures are extremely volatile quarter to quarter (reporting/spinoff-related distortions), and the most recent quarter missed EPS estimates (0.39 vs 0.42). The announced $3.4B 2026-2030 infrastructure investment plan is a genuine positive long-term catalyst. Analyst rating is C/Hold with some sell ratings.
ranked_picks.rationale: Positive long-term infrastructure catalyst but noisy recent fundamentals and a comparatively low 3.0 R:R make this a lower-conviction pick.
flags: Low RRRatio (3.0) relative to peers though not flagged WeakRR by the system

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Olin's revenue has declined in three of the last four quarters and net income has remained negative/volatile, with the company still posting negative EPS in most trailing quarters (though beating low estimates in 4 of 6). The major catalyst is the newly announced Huntsman merger, but headlines note shares sliding as investors weigh deal terms and integration risk. Analyst rating is C+/Hold.
ranked_picks.rationale: Weak standalone fundamentals; the merger is a real catalyst but the market's initial reaction was negative, so this is ranked as a lower-conviction, speculative holding.
flags: Merger-related uncertainty (Huntsman deal) could drive outsized volatility independent of the technical setup

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Huntsman has posted negative EPS in most trailing quarters with deeply volatile net income, though it has beaten/met low estimates recently. The major catalyst is the newly announced all-cash merger with Olin, but 'Huntsman Stock Is Sliding After Its Big Olin Merger Deal' indicates a negative initial market reaction. Analyst rating is C+/Hold.
ranked_picks.rationale: Same merger situation as OLN from the other side; weak standalone fundamentals and a negative initial deal reaction keep this near the bottom of the list despite the extreme headline R:R.
flags: StopSanityFlag true (RR 33.81 likely reflects an unusually tight stop)

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Capital Southwest's earnings record is roughly balanced (3 beats, 3 misses, 2 met over the last 8 quarters), and the most recent quarter showed declining revenue (-12.8%) and net income (-16.5%) after a strong prior quarter. News flow is routine with no material catalysts. Analyst rating is B+/Buy.
ranked_picks.rationale: Lowest-conviction pick on the list: the system's own WeakRR flag indicates the 2.27:1 reward/risk fell short of the minimum threshold even after support/resistance refinement, and fundamentals are only middling.
flags: WeakRR true - R:R (2.27) fell short of minimum after refinement; size down or skip
